_doc: The stack-expression registry — HOW each stack (vllm/ollama/litellm-cloud/custom-server) EXPRESSES each capability: the launch FLAG fragment (`serve`) it emits and the per-request FIELD (`use`) it sends. CAPABILITY-RESOLUTION-DESIGN-CORRECTED.md §2. registry-is-truth (AGENTS.md rule 8): this FILE is the single source; runtime/capabilities/resolver.py loads it at import (fail-loud). Adding a NEW stack = adding ONE top-level entry HERE (no code edit); teaching an existing stack a new capability = adding one capability key under it. The keys under each stack are the `serve_ref` values from capability_types.json. A `serve` value is a LIST of TEMPLATE fragments — a token may be a {placeholder} the resolver fills from the resolved family/model (e.g. {tool_parser}, {chat_template}). The resolver concatenates the serve-fragments of a model's owned capabilities IN CANONICAL ORDER (`_serve_order` in resolver.py) and appends config.extra_flags verbatim — reproducing today's hand-authored flag list. SAFE-half NOTE: this is DATA only; the SAFE-half resolver reproduces TODAY exactly — the `reasoning` capability emits the EXISTING `chat_template_nothink.jinja` (the design's swap-to-a-thinking-template is the LATER LIVE phase, NOT here). The `~`-path is emitted VERBATIM (literal `~`); serveconfig.args_for's existing expanduser applies identically at launch, so behaviour is byte-preserved. custom-server is the escape hatch: its capabilities are served by a bespoke script, NOT flag-generated — keyless/custom embedders (embed-pplx, embed-jina-v4) live here and are deliberately NOT regenerated.

vllm._note: the config-driven vLLM stack (ops/serve_model.sh -> ops/cli/serveconfig.py args_for). Reproduces the live launch flags for the config-driven services (chat-4b/2b/08b, embed-bge/jina-v5/qwen3).

reasoning.use: body.chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking (LIVE-phase; SAFE half launches the existing template verbatim)

structured_output.use: body.response_format={type:json_schema,...} (vLLM >=0.21 guided decode is automatic — no launch flag)

vision._note: emitted only for the lm-only vision mode (text RHM skips the vision tower); a full-vision model emits nothing here.

context_window._note: context_window is a MODEL runtime param, emitted by serveconfig.args_for today from config.max_model_len BEFORE the flags[] block — NOT part of the family/stack flag generation in the SAFE half. Recorded here for completeness; serve_flags() generates the post-runtime-param flag list only.

ollama._note: the ollama-native stack (already wired in fabric/transport.py:ollama_native_transport). Capabilities are declared via /api/show; expression is per-request, not launch-flag.

structured_output.use: body.format (loose; cloud-inconsistent -> validate/retry is the guarantee)

litellm-cloud._note: the LiteLLM/cloud stack — capabilities read from model_info.supports_*; many are unknown -> declared. No launch flags (the provider runs the model).

custom-server._note: the ESCAPE HATCH stack — capabilities served by a bespoke serve_*.sh / serve_*.py, NOT flag-generated. embed-pplx (ops/serve_pplx_embed.py) + embed-jina-v4 (serve_jina_v4.py) live here: vLLM cannot load their arch, so they are deliberately NOT regenerated. serve_flags() returns [] for a custom-server model (its launch is its own script).
